violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags were lacking the date of last sold/ served. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025

-Raw hamburgers were observed stored above hamburger buns in grill cooler -Raw fish was observed stored above frozen cooked shrimp -Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above cooked bacon. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025

The food contact surfaces of the slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025

The food contact surfaces of the dishes in the main dishmachine were not sanitized as evidence by the failure of the thermolabel turning black. Establishment must use 3 bay sink to sanitize dishes in the main kitchen. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025

The presence of small flies in the bar area is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of pests must be monitored and controlled by eliminating contributing factors and exclusion. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed with an accumulation of debris: -interior of lowboy refrigeration/ reach-in coolers -exterior of kitchen equipment (oven, fryers) -walk-in cooler fanguards -walk-in cooler shelving New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025
Allergen Advisory is lacking on menus. Sign placed at host stand during time of inspection. Revised menus to include Allergen Advisory statement. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2024

Shellstock tags for littlenecks are not being maintained with the last sold/served date. Shellstock tags to be kept on file for 90 days in chronological order. Tags must stay with shellstock until batch or bag is completely used/ sold. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024

Pasta observed at 50 degrees Fahrenheit in cold hold unit (food observed filled beyond load limit and next to grill). Per staff, food was recently placed. Food above load limit was relocated to freezer to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024

Previously cooked, cooled and frozen french onion soup was thawed and observed with a date exceeding 7 days. Establishment to redate/ add pull date once removed from freezer to cold holding. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024

Vacuum packaged salmon was observed at 38 degrees Fahrenheit with packaging still intact. Vacuum packaged fish must be removed from packaging prior to thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2024

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidence by live insects observed during time of inspection. Establishment to work with Pest Control to eliminate pest issue. Establishment to exclude stagnant water and food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024
(3) 2 door lowboys out of service prior to inspection. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024
Cutting board on the line was observed very scratched and scored. Equipment to be resurfaced or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024
